summary: Tested gender/division features: onboarding gender selector, theme switching, social media step, dashboard theme-aware elements, colleges page gender-aware header/buttons, and backend profile/colleges APIs.

ui_bugs.issue: Several dashboard elements (weekly digest banner, email icons, follow-up badges) still use hardcoded orange Tailwind classes instead of theme-aware colors. They remain orange even in Women's mode. Only the key themed elements (header text, Find Colleges button, accent label) use theme colors correctly.

issues: Header shows 'COLLEGE DIRECTORY' not 'College Directory' - this is fine, uppercase CSS

action_items: Minor: Consider updating hardcoded orange classes in Dashboard (weekly digest banner, follow-up badges, email dot indicators) to use theme.btnClass or theme.accent for full theme consistency
critical_code_review_comments: Dashboard.js has many hardcoded 'bg-orange-500', 'text-orange-*' classes that are not theme-aware (lines 109, 179, 187, 220, 231, 281, 358, 367). The key UX elements (header subtitle, Find Colleges button) do use theme correctly but secondary elements don't. Low priority issue.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All gender/division features working: onboarding step 0 shows Men's/Women's cards, selecting Women's sets data-division=womens (rose theme), selecting Men's sets orange theme. Step 4 shows Instagram/Twitter inputs. Skip navigates to /dashboard. Dashboard shows correct subtitle (Women's | Basketball Scholarship Tracker vs European |...). Find Colleges button uses theme accent color. CollegesPage shows WOMEN'S DIVISION header for womens and COLLEGE DIRECTORY for mens. All 230 colleges show for both. Backend /api/colleges 200, /api/profile GET/PUT all pass including basketball_gender field.
